# UniversalMailCleaner

UniversalMailCleaner is a local-first Windows desktop app for cleaning IMAP and Gmail mailboxes. It uses PySide6 and supports rule-based cleanup, safe trash mode, undo for trash operations, large-mail scans, Gmail API OAuth2 accounts, Gmail labels, Drive file cleanup, and scheduled cleanup runs.

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/doc-bricks/UniversalMailCleaner
- Organization: doc-bricks
- License: MIT
- Main app entry point: `mail_imap_cleaner_v1.py`
- Core modules: `imap_client.py`, `gmail_service.py`, `workers.py`, `models.py`, `scheduler_widget.py`
- Tests: `tests/`

## Audience

- **Privacy-conscious Windows users** who want to clean Gmail or IMAP mailboxes without handing their inbox to a cloud service.
- **Gmail power users** who need label-aware cleanup, Drive file scanning, large-mail detection, and OAuth2-based management — all running locally.
- **IMAP users** on GMX, Outlook, T-Online, and other standard providers who want rule-based age/sender/subject filters with safe trash mode.
- **Freelancers and small businesses** looking for scheduled mailbox maintenance without a subscription or hosted service.
- **Developers** building or extending PySide6 Windows desktop workflows for email management, IMAP automation, or Gmail API integration.

## Search Phrases

- `local Gmail cleanup tool`
- `IMAP mailbox cleaner Windows`
- `Gmail label cleanup local app`
- `large email finder Gmail IMAP`
- `safe trash mode email deletion`
- `undoable mailbox cleanup`
- `Gmail Drive storage cleaner desktop`
- `PySide6 email management app`
- `local-first Gmail and IMAP cleaner`
- `Windows email cleaner no subscription`

## Last-checked: 2026-06-11

## Safety Model

- Safe mode moves messages to trash by default.
- Undo is available for safe-mode delete actions.
- IMAP uses SSL connections.
- Passwords are stored through the OS keyring when available.
- Gmail API access uses OAuth2 with local credentials and token files.

- MailProcessor: https://github.com/doc-bricks/MailProcessor
- UniversalDocsGrabber: https://github.com/doc-bricks/UniversalDocsGrabber
